title: Dasystephana makinoi
intro: Dasystephana makinoi, also known as Japanese rose, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Japan and Taiwan. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Dasystephana makinoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dasystephana makinoi is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
